title: Kyiv Expresses Doubts Over Russia's Commitment to Honor Easter Ceasefire
detail: Ukrainian officials expressed lingering skepticism over Moscow's commitment to uphold the agreed Orthodox Easter ceasefire, despite Putin's public announcement endorsing the truce. Kyiv signaled doubt that Russia will observe the pause in fighting, citing Moscow's history of violating ceasefire commitments and continued military activity along the front. The skepticism underscores the fragile nature of the Easter truce arrangement, even as diplomatic momentum toward a broader peace framework had appeared to be building in prior days.

title: Orthodox Easter Ceasefire Enters Active Implementation — First Halt in Hostilities
detail: The Russia-Ukraine Orthodox Easter ceasefire moved from announcement to active implementation on April 11, 2026, marking the first halt in hostilities since peace talks accelerated. The ceasefire represents a significant milestone as the first agreed bilateral pause in the full-scale invasion. Both sides had previously agreed to the Easter truce — though Ukrainian officials continued to express skepticism over Russia's commitment to hold the line, citing Moscow's history of ceasefire violations. The transition from announcement to implementation is being closely watched as a potential gauge of whether broader diplomatic progress is achievable.
